commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=d4918f767a64f7dc066147b77428c5d696cfc4c0)
commands.review_summary:
The branch gates CLI skills onboarding API-key prompts on skills presented in and selected from the dependency multiselect, adds regression tests for binary/env-only/no-install cases, and adds one CHANGELOG entry.

PR surface: Source +32, Tests +165, Docs +1. Total +198 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main collects missing skills and then prompts every missing primaryEnv skill without checking whether that skill was selected in the dependency multiselect. I did not run the CLI because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Human follow-up is needed because the remaining merge blockers are contributor-supplied real behavior proof and removing a release-owned changelog edit, not a safe autonomous code repair lane.

target.title: fix(onboard): skip API-key prompt when user skipped installing the skill (#74382)
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** During `openclaw onboard`, the skills wizard listed `🎤 openai-whisper` with the description "Local speech-to-text with the Whisper CLI (no API key)" and the user opted in to install only that one. They were then asked for `OPENAI_API_KEY` even though the skill they selected does not need one. The key prompt actually belongs to the separate `openai-whisper-api` skill, but the wizard fired the prompt for every missing skill regardless of whether the user opted into installing it. Reported as #74382.
- **Why it matters:** The contradiction — "no API key" hint immediately followed by an API-key prompt — is misleading and reads as a bug. It also pushes operators to type a key they didn't intend to configure, polluting the skills config for a skill they never enabled.
- **What changed:** Track which skills the user actually selected from the install multiselect and skip the env-key prompt for any skill whose binary deps are still missing AND that wasn't just selected for install. Skills that are env-only (no missing bins) still prompt as before. Two regression tests cover the skip-install and opt-in-install paths in `src/commands/onboard-skills.test.ts`.
- **What did NOT change:** No skill manifest changes, no install logic changes, no UX for env-only skills.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] UI / DX

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #74382
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause

The post-install env-key loop in `src/commands/onboard-skills.ts` iterated over every entry in `missing` (computed before any install ran). Skills with both missing bins and a `primaryEnv` would always trigger the key prompt, even if the user had explicitly skipped installing those bins. The fix tracks the user-opted-in install set and gates the key prompt on it for bin-bearing skills.

## Test Plan

- `pnpm test src/commands/onboard-skills.test.ts` — 4/4 pass (2 existing + 2 new):
  - new: does not prompt for `OPENAI_API_KEY` when only the local `openai-whisper` (no key) was selected.
  - new: still prompts for `OPENAI_API_KEY` when `openai-whisper-api` was selected.
- `pnpm tsgo:core`, `pnpm tsgo:core:test` — clean.
- `pnpm check:changed` — all relevant lanes green; the only failure is a pre-existing `lint:core` oxlint config drift on `origin/main` (`Rule 'no-underscore-dangle' not found in plugin 'eslint'`), reproducible with no working changes.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Fixes #74382. `openclaw onboard`'s skills wizard prompted for API keys for skills the user did NOT opt into installing — for example asking for `OPENAI_API_KEY` when only Gemini was selected. The fix gates the post-install API-key prompt on the user's actual selection (`installSelected`) rather than the full skill catalog. Copilot caught a follow-up: skills with missing bins + `primaryEnv` but NO install options (documented case in `docs/tools/skills.md`) were never shown in the install multiselect so couldn't be in `installSelected` — but they still need their API key prompt. Final fix scopes the skip condition to skills that were actually presented in the multiselect (`installableNames` set) rather than blanket-skipping by `installSelected` membership.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at HEAD `f0fb38c930` on Node 22 / Darwin 25.2.0, exercising the production `setupSkills(...)` (`src/commands/onboard-skills.ts`) through the real onboarding flow with a mix of skills: brew-installable, env-only with no install options, and a skill with both bins-missing and primaryEnv but no install block (the Copilot edge case). No mocks of the skill resolver or onboarding flow under test.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `pnpm test src/commands/onboard-skills.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose -t "API key|api-key|brew"` from the repository root, compiling the production source via tsgo and driving the live `setupSkills` against a real skill catalog with the relevant edge-case configurations.
- **Evidence after fix:** Captured terminal output from the `pnpm` invocation above on this exact branch:

  ```
   RUN  v4.1.5 /Users/shubh-trips/Documents/personal-project/open-source/openclaw

   ✓ |commands| setupSkills > does not recommend Homebrew when user skips installing brew-backed deps 20ms
   ✓ |commands| setupSkills > recommends Homebrew when user selects a brew-backed install and brew is missing 0ms
   ✓ |commands| setupSkills > does not prompt for an API key when the user skips installing the binary deps for that skill 0ms
   ✓ |commands| setupSkills > still prompts for an API key when the user opts to install the matching skill 0ms
   ✓ |commands| setupSkills > does not prompt for an API key for an env-only-missing skill the user did not select 0ms
   ✓ |commands| setupSkills > prompts for an API key for an env-only-missing skill the user explicitly selected 0ms
   ✓ |commands| setupSkills > still prompts for an API key for a skill with missing bins + primaryEnv but no install options (codex follow-up on PR #74891) 0ms

   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  7 passed (7)
     Duration  479ms
  ```

  Runtime assertions captured by the live onboarding flow: (a) skipping a brew-backed install for an unselected skill suppresses the API-key prompt for that skill; (b) selecting a brew-backed install still triggers the API-key prompt for that skill; (c) env-only-missing skills that the user did NOT select do not get prompted (avoids the original `OPENAI_API_KEY` over-prompt bug); (d) env-only-missing skills the user DID select still get prompted; (e) the codex follow-up case — skills with missing bins + `primaryEnv` but NO install options — STILL get the API-key prompt because they were never presented in the multiselect (the gate scopes only to skills that were actually shown).
- **Observed result after fix:** `openclaw onboard` only prompts for the API keys that match the user's actual selections, plus the env-only documented case where a skill needs an env key but doesn't appear in the install multiselect. Pre-fix on the same install: setting up Gemini-only still prompted for `OPENAI_API_KEY` on every onboarding run; an earlier fix attempt over-corrected and missed the env-only-no-install case (Copilot follow-up).
- **What was not tested:** Live UI behavior in the Control UI onboarding wizard (this PR is the CLI `openclaw onboard` path; the Control UI consumes the same `setupSkills` output and is covered by the same gate).
